title: Insilico Medicine partners with US firm to build AI model for human longevity
description: Insilico, already responsible for an outsized share of AI-generated drug candidates entering the clinic, formalized a US partnership and longevity advisory board to extend its generative platform from disease-specific assets to aging itself. This is the field's most concrete bridge yet between mainstream AI drug discovery and explicit longevity targeting.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 delivers ~85% hsCRP knockdown — NLRP3 inflammaging druggable
description: BioAge's oral NLRP3 inhibitor cut median hsCRP by 83–86% across both dose cohorts with 87–93% of participants reaching <2 mg/L and no serious adverse events. Combined with this week's platelet/HuR data, NLRP3 emerges as the most clinically advanced inflammaging target heading into a Phase 2 CV risk trial in H1 2026.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1 confirms human 7-ketocholesterol clearance — atherosclerosis target validated
description: First-in-human data show UDP-003 dose-dependently increases urinary 7KC excretion with no serious side effects and a clean 3-hour half-life. This is the first clinical proof that an aging-damage molecule long thought central to atherosclerosis can be safely targeted and removed from humans.

title: Sheba Medical: biological-age gap predicts mortality, each year adds 15% risk
description: Israeli study finds every additional year that biological age exceeds chronological age is associated with a 15% higher mortality risk and 6% more hospitalizations, even adjusting for BMI, smoking, and hypertension. The data hardens the clinical utility case for bio-age clocks as a screening biomarker, not just a research tool.

title: Buffalo TTP protein restoration reverses frailty in old mice — translational drug screen underway
description: University at Buffalo showed engineering elderly mice to maintain TTP (tristetraprolin) — an RNA-binding protein that tags pro-inflammatory transcripts for degradation — restored grip strength, gait, bone health, and immune profile. Blackshear has already begun screening small molecules that boost TTP expression, opening a new node in the inflammaging druggable landscape.

title: Insilico taps US partner to build AI model for human longevity
description: Insilico Medicine announced a US partnership and dedicated longevity advisory board to extend its generative AI drug-discovery platform toward aging biology, signaling deeper integration of mainstream AI drug discovery with explicit geroscience.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 cuts hsCRP 83-86%, no SAEs
description: BioAge Labs reported positive Phase 1 data for oral NLRP3 inhibitor BGE-102 with 87-93% of participants reaching hsCRP <2 mg/L. Phase 2 cardiovascular risk trial planned for H1 2026 with data expected H2 2026.

title: Cyclarity UDP-003 Phase 1 safely clears 7-ketocholesterol in humans
description: Randomized, double-blind Phase 1 of cyclodextrin drug UDP-003 in 84 participants showed dose-dependent urinary 7-ketocholesterol excretion, 3-hour half-life, and no serious adverse events — first human evidence that 7KC, a key driver of atherosclerosis, can be pharmacologically removed.

title: Sheba Medical: bio-age gap predicts mortality and hospitalization risk
description: Sheba Medical Center study finds each additional year that biological age exceeds chronological age associates with 15% higher mortality and 6% more hospitalizations, after adjusting for BMI, smoking, and hypertension.

title: Buffalo team boosts TTP and aging mice get stronger, more mobile
description: University at Buffalo / Aging and Disease paper shows engineering elderly mice to maintain TTP (tristetraprolin) expression improved grip, endurance, bone health, and immune profile; early small-molecule TTP-expression boosters are now in screening.

title: Retro Biosciences raise values longevity startup at $1.8B
description: Retro CEO Joe Betts-LaCroix confirmed at STAT Breakthrough Summit that the autophagy-focused Alzheimer's Phase 1 is going "super good" with no dose-limiting toxicities and initial data expected around August, anchoring protein-clearance as a major venture-backed longevity modality.

title: Life Biosciences ER-100 begins dosing — first human OSK partial-reprogramming trial
description: Sinclair-founded Life Biosciences received FDA Phase 1 clearance in January and began dosing glaucoma patients with ER-100 in March, marking the first human attempt to reverse cellular biological age via OSK partial reprogramming.

title: Fight Aging weekly digest: ATF4 suppression extends Drosophila lifespan
description: Drosophila study finds chronic ATF4 activation reduces lifespan while ATF4 knockdown extends lifespan, upregulating proteostasis and DNA-repair pathways and reframing integrated stress response as context-dependent for aging.

title: Gut luminal exosomes from old mice impair barrier integrity in young recipients
description: Multi-omic analysis finds fecal exosomes from old mice carry proteins and microRNAs linked to insulin resistance and gut-barrier disruption, transferring metabolic dysfunction when administered to young mice.

title: KHL Foundation launches 'Rejuvenation Cocktail' gene-therapy program for over-60s
description: Kenneth Scott / Helga Sands foundation announced an open-access program offering combination gene therapies targeting muscle, brain, and metabolic aging in older adults — a controversial first push to bring rejuvenation gene therapies directly to consumers.

title: Hallmarks of Aging philosophy review: framework structures geroscience without consensus
description: Philosophy-of-science review argues the Hallmarks of Aging has become the field's dominant organizing framework — stabilizing collaboration and funding even as theoretical disagreements persist about aging's root cause.

title: Mitochondrial aging linked to phosphatidylcholine loss; supplementation reverses fragmentation
description: Nature Communications paper finds phosphatidylcholine, the most abundant lipid in mitochondrial membranes, declines with age; dietary PC supplementation reversed mitochondrial fragmentation in pmt-1 and sams-1 worms and choline protected human fibroblasts from metformin-induced complex I inhibition.

title: Common diabetes drug linked to 'exceptional longevity' in women
description: New observational analysis links a commonly prescribed diabetes drug with substantially elevated odds of reaching exceptional old age in women, reinforcing the case for metformin-class agents as candidate geroprotectors.

title: Low-intensity pulsed ultrasound ameliorates sarcopenia via gut microbiota in aged mice
description: Naturally aged mice receiving abdominal low-intensity pulsed ultrasound showed improved muscle strength and mass alongside shifts in gut microbiota toward anti-inflammatory taxa, suggesting a non-invasive gut-muscle axis intervention.

title: Metabolomic age delta plus APOE ε4 confers very high dementia risk in UK Biobank
description: UK Biobank analysis shows higher metabolomic age delta independently predicts dementia risk and earlier onset; combined with APOE ε4 status, risk stratification becomes particularly steep.

title: Age-associated B cells emerge as therapeutic target for autoimmune diseases
description: Review consolidates evidence that age-associated B cells (ABCs) — a heterogeneous subset accumulating with age — drive multiple autoimmune disorders and may serve as both biomarker and druggable target.

title: Tuberculosis treatment in old mice reveals immunometabolic dysfunction
description: Aged mice cleared TB more slowly after treatment, with altered T cell populations and mitochondrial dysfunction implicated, underscoring how immune aging undermines anti-infective drug response.

title: Sex differences shape immune aging — one-size-fits-all medicine challenged
description: Review highlights the morbidity-mortality paradox, sex-chromosome and sex-hormone effects, and differences in adaptive and innate immunity that argue for sex-stratified aging interventions.

title: Rubedo RLS-1496 enters Phase 1 for senescence-driven skin disease
description: LongeVC portfolio company Rubedo Life Sciences is now enrolling Phase 1 in plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and actinic keratoses for its precision-senolytic RLS-1496, extending the field's pivot from broad zombie-cell clearance to indication-specific targeting.

title: CRISPR-Cas12a2 system kills cells on demand via RNA targeting
description: Nature paper reports a Cas12a2 platform that programmably destroys cells expressing a target RNA — including cancerous and virus-infected cells — with minimal off-target activity, opening a precision-cytotoxicity tool with potential geroscience applications.

title: Stanford simulated-microgravity bioreactor validates as days-long aging test bench
description: Stanford team exposed human blood cells to a rotating wall bioreactor and matched the aging signatures to the same donors' actual blood up to 9 years later — validating simulated microgravity as a rapid screening platform for anti-aging interventions.

title: Cellular senescence review: zombie cells aren't all bad
description: Major review challenges the broad 'kill all senescent cells' framing, cataloging beneficial roles of transient senescent cells in wound healing and tissue plasticity and arguing for precision targeting of harmful subsets.

title: XPRIZE Healthspan names top 100 teams advancing healthy aging
description: XPRIZE Healthspan disclosed the top 100 advancing teams in its $101M competition to demonstrate measurable functional rejuvenation in older adults, narrowing the field of testable interventions heading into next-round evaluations.

title: AlzeCure ACD856 Phase Ib completed in Alzheimer's pipeline
description: AlzeCure Pharma completed Phase Ib study of NeuroRestore candidate ACD856, advancing a TrkA/B-positive modulator approach to neurodegeneration that intersects directly with brain aging biology.

title: Seragon SRN-901 extends median mouse lifespan in published study
description: Seragon Biosciences disclosed lifespan-extension data for SRN-901 in murine studies, adding another small molecule to the testable geroprotector pipeline and expanding the post-ITP candidate roster.

title: Alex Zhavoronkov named to Business Insider's most innovative leaders list
description: Insilico Medicine CEO Alex Zhavoronkov recognized for advances in AI-driven drug discovery, with Insilico now responsible for an estimated 70-80% of all AI-generated drugs entering clinical trials.
